messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show resources command do?

The show resources command shows hardware resource consumption on the switch. Resource data is updated every 10 seconds. Hardware resource consumption information is shown for: • TCAM entries • Policers • L4 Port Ranges Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show resources [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip local-proxy-arp command do?

The ip local-proxy-arp command enables local proxy ARP on the specified interface. Local proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces and on VLAN interfaces. To enable local proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no ip local-proxy-arp command disables local proxy ARP on the specified interface. uplink-passthrough (For 10000 Switch series only) Enables local proxy ARP passth rough to uplink members. NOTE When enabling ip local-proxy-arp uplink- passthrough, ip local-proxy-arp will be imp licitly enabled. Syntax:
- no ip local-proxy-arp
- ip local-proxy-arp 223.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip proxy-arp command do?

The ip proxy-arp command enables proxy ARP for the specified Layer 3 interface. Proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces, LAG interfaces, and VLAN interfaces. It is disabled by default. To enable proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no form of this command disables proxy ARP for the specified interface. Syntax: no ip proxy-arp.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Specifies the address on which to enable BFD in IPv4 format (x. x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>. Syntax: no bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all active OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 o spfv3 bfd. Syntax: no bfd all-interfaces.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the bfd echo disable command do?

The bfd echo disable command disables support for BFD echo packets. Echo packet support is enabled by default. The no form of this command enables support for BFD echo packets. NOTE Toggling this feature on 8100, 8325, 8360, 9300, 9300S, 10040 switches may cause route flapping. NOTE BFD IPv6 Echo is not supported. Syntax: no bfd echo disable.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD echo packets. The no form of this command removes the configured BFD echo packets interval. If the interval is not set, the default interval is used. NOTE BFD IPv6 Echo is not supported. Specifies the minimum reception interval in milliseconds. A valu e of 0 means that the switch does not support reception of BFD echo packets. Range: 0, 50 to 1000. Default: 500. <INTERVAL>. Syntax:
- no bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
- bfd min-echo-receive-interval 283.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command removes the configured BFD minimum interval on an interface. If the interval is not set, the default interval is used. Specifies the minimum receive interval in milliseconds. A value of 0 means that the switch does not support reception of BFD c ontrol packets. Range: 100 to 20000. Default: 3000. <INTERVAL>. Syntax: no bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor ebgp-multihop command do?

The neighbor ebgp-multihop command enables BGP to establish a session with external peers residing on networks that are not directly connected. By default, BGP can only establish sessions with external BGP peers that are directly connected. If external BGP peer are not connected directly, then ebgp-multihop is enabled to initiate the connection with that external peer. If ebgp-multihop is disabled and external BGP peers are indirectly connected, then BGP peer session is not established. The no form of this command disables the peer ebgp-multihop feature. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> <ttl> Enables BGP fast peering session deactivation. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ebgp-multihop <ttl>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vrf <VRF-NAME> command do?

The vrf <VRF-NAME> command creates a VRF instance named <VRF-NAME> and then enters its context. Use default for <VRF-NAME> to enter the default VRF configure context. Except for the default VRF, the no form of the command deletes the named VRF instance and any IP configuration for interfaces or SVI linked to default VRF. The default VRF cannot be deleted and a warning is given if attempted. To erase the Route-Distinguisher and Route-Targets, enter the default VRF context and delete them manually one by one. Specifies the VRF name. Range: Up to 32 alphanumeric chara cters. The mgmt VRF cannot be used. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax:
- no vrf <VRF-NAME>
- vrf 494.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip pim-bidir command do?

The ip pim-bidir command enables or disables PIM-Bidir in the designated interface. This command works in the interface context, including loopback. The IP address must be configured on the interface to enable BIDIR-PIM. Public BIDIR-PIM commands 496 Enables PIM Bidirectional on the interface. Disables PIM Bidirectional on the interface. Syntax: ip pim-bidir [enable | disable].

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE> command changes the maximum time, in seconds, before the router transmits the initial PIM hello message on the current interface. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets it to default. The default value is 5. Configures the given value as the hello interval. Range: <0-5>. <DELAY-VALUE>. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command changes the frequency at which the router transmits PIM hello messages on the current subnet. The router uses hello packets to inform neighbor routers of its presence and to comput the hello hold time. Hello hold time informs neighbor routers estimated wait time for the next hello packet from the router. Shortening the hello interval reduces the hello hold time. Default is set to 30 seconds. The no form of this command resets the frequency of PIM hello messages back to the default value on the current subnet. Public ip pim-bidir hello-interval 499 Configures the given value as hello interval. Range: 1-300 seco nds. • The default value (30 seconds) covers most deployment needs; change only for explicit convergence or timing requirements. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value>.

The syntax of the ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command is: no ip pim-bidir hello-interval <interval-value>

document_title: Command Line Interface Reference Guide For 8320, 8325, 8325H, 8325P Switch

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command do?

The ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command configures the Hello Hold time (in seconds) advertised in PIM Hello messages on this interface; this overrides the default value derived from the current hello-interval. By default, the hold time that a neighbor uses before declaring this router down is automatically calculated (typically a multiple of the configured hello-interval). Manually configuring the hello-holdtime allows finer control of neighbor timeout detection. A smaller value can speed convergence (neighbors age out faster if hellos stop) but increases sensitivity to transient loss; a larger value provides stability at the cost of slower failure detection. Syntax: no ip pim-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command do?

The IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-interval <interval-value> command changes the frequency at which the router transmits PIM hello messages on the current subnet. The router uses hello packets to inform neighbor routers of its presence and to comput the hello hold time. Hello hold time informs neighbor routers estimated wait time for the next hello packet from the router. Shortening the hello interval reduces the hello hold time. Default is set to 30 seconds. The no form of this command resets the frequency of PIM hello messages back to the default value on the current subnet. Public IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-interval 514 Configures the given value as hello interval. Range: 1-300 seco nds. • The default value (30 seconds) covers most deployment needs; change only for explicit convergence or timing requirements. Syntax: no IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-interval <interval-value>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command do?

The IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value> command configures the Hello Hold time (in seconds) advertised in PIM Hello messages on this interface; this overrides the default value derived from the current hello-interval. By default, the hold time that a neighbor uses before declaring this router down is automatically calculated (typically a multiple of the configured hello-interval). Manually configuring the hello-holdtime allows finer control of neighbor timeout detection. A smaller value can speed convergence (neighbors age out faster if hellos stop) but increases sensitivity to transient loss; a larger value provides stability at the cost of slower failure detection. In chatty networks where there is a chance of Hello packets being lost, configuring a higher hold time reduces the likelihood of adjacency loss. Syntax: no IPv6 pim6-bidir hello-holdtime <holdtime-value>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 pim6-bidir lan-prune-delay command do?

The IPv6 pim6-bidir lan-prune-delay command enables the LAN prune delay option on the current interface. With lan-prune-delay enabled, the router informs downstream neighbors on the wait time before pruning a flow after receiving a prune request. The no form of this command disables the LAN prune delay option on the current interface. Syntax: no IPv6 pim6-bidir lan-prune-delay.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command do?

The apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command applies a policy to the current physical interface port or LAG or VLAN context. Only one direction of a policy can be applied to an interface or VLAN at a time, thus using the apply command on an interface or VLAN with an already-applied policy of the same direction will replace the currently applied policy. NOTE The VLAN context supports the in and out directions, which apply to both bridged and routed traffic. The Interface VLAN context only supports the routed-in direction which applies only to routed traffic. The no form of this command removes a policy from the interface or VLANspecified by the current context. Specifies the policy to apply. <POLICY-NAME> in out Selects the inbound (ingress) traffic direction. Selects the outbound (egress) traffic direction. Syntax: no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the class mac command do?

The class mac command creates or modifies a MAC traffic class to match specified packets. Class is composed of one or more class entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. With this command, each class can classify traffic based on MAC header information. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a MAC traffic class (use no with the class command) or an individual MAC traffic class entry (use no with the sequence number). Specifies create or modify a MAC class. Specifies the name of this class. Specifies a sequence number for the class entry. Optional. Rang e: 1-4294967295. {match|ignore} Creates a rule to match or ignore specified packets. comment Stores the remaining entered text as a class comment. Syntax: class mac <CLASS-NAME>.

The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax:
- ip dns host 982
- no ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erps ring <RINGID> command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Public erps ring 1002 Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> This command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <PORT0> <PORT1> <ifname> This command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command do?

The erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Required, specifies the ID of the ring. Range: 1-239 <RINGID> <ID> In ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. Syntax: erps ring <RINGID> instance <ID> revertive.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command do?

The https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command this command enables or disables the Firmware Site Distribution server listening on port 9443. The firmware site distribution allows you to use a switch to distribute a firmware image file to other switches in the same network. This prevents the switches from connecting to the cloud or an external network to download a firmware image file. On enabling the firmware site distribution, it exposes a REST endpoint that allows the switches to download a switch primary or secondary firmware image. 1 NOTE As per the limitation, up to two switches can download the firmware image simultaneously. This endpoint is to be used along with REST /firmware endpoint to handle the firmware download and installation process. The no form of this command disables the firmware site distribution server. Syntax: no https-server rest firmware-site-distribution.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip icmp unreachable command do?

The ip icmp unreachable command enables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages on the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. The unreachable host address originates from the failed packed. Default setting. The no form of this command disables the sending of ICMPv4 and ICMPv6 destination unreachable messages from the switch to a source host when a specific host is unreachable. This command does not prevent other hosts from sending an ICMP unreachable message. Syntax:
- no ip icmp unreachable
- ip icmp unreachable 1296.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command do?

The ip igmp snooping all-vlans command enable IGMP snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables IGMP snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the ip igmp snooping all-vlans command is issued. When IGMP snooping is enabled globally, IGMP snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global IGMP snooping, allows IGMP snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level IGMP snooping settings. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip tcp mss <VALUE> command do?

The ip tcp mss <VALUE> command sets the TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) MSS (Maximum Segment Size) for an interface. When configured, the MSS option in the TCP header is set in the TCP handshake for negotiation during connection establishment. Once the TCP session is established, changing the TCP MSS value on the interface will not affect the existing session. Public ip tcp mss 1394 The no form of this command removes the TCP MSS configuration from the interface. NOTE TCP MSS is supported on ROP, SVI, subinterface, and L3 GRE tunnel interface. Specifies the IPv4 TCP MSS in bytes. Range: 68 to 65495. <VALUE>. Syntax: no ip tcp mss <VALUE>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip unnumbered <ifname> command do?

The ip unnumbered <ifname> command the IP unnumbered feature allows the switch to process IP packets without configuring a unique IP address on an interface by telling the router to use the IP address of another selected interface as the address for that link. Once set, this port will borrow the user-configured primary IPv4 address from an lender interface and use that IP address in its L3 control plane exchange. The no version of this command removes IP unnumbered support for this port. Once unset, this port will remove borrowed IPv4 address. Name of the interface. Syntax: no ip unnumbered <ifname>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip neighbor-flood command do?

The ip neighbor-flood command enables VLAN flooding for the specified VLAN interface when a neighbor link goes down. The no form of this command disables VLAN flooding for the specified VLAN interface. Public IP Neighbor Flood commands 1490 Displays the status of IP Neighbor Flood on the specified interface along with other interface-related attributes. Specifies the interface name (for example, vlan30). Optional. <IFNAME> Displays the interfaces on which IP Neighbor Flood is enabled. Displays the current running configuration. Syntax: ip neighbor-flood.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME> command do?

The ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME> command creates an IP Service Level Agreement (SLA) profile and switches to the config-ip-sla context. The no form of this command deletes an IP-SLA profile. By default, all profile use the default VRF (default). Specifies an IP-SLA profile name. Length: 1 to 64 characters. <IP-SLA-NAME> Public ip-sla 1502 Selects the IP-SLA responder. The responder can be configured for udp-echo, tcp-connect, udp-jitter-voip type. It requires the SLA name, SLA type, and port number as arguments. The no form of this command removes the IP-SLA responder. Specifies the IP-SLA responder name. Length: 1 to 64 chara cters. <SLA-NAME> udp-echo tcp-connect Enables responder for udp-echo probes. Selects TCP connect as the IP-SLA test mechanism. udp-jitter-voip Selects VOIP jitter as the IP-SLA test mechanism. Syntax: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> (udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip).

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command do?

The ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command sets the local IP address of a GRE tunnel. This address identifies the tunnel interface for routing. It must be on the same subnet as the tunnel address assigned on the remote device. The no form of this command deletes the local IP address assigned to a GRE tunnel. Specifies the tunnel IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading ze ros. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192. 168.5.100. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. <IPv4-ADDR> <MASK>. Syntax: no ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>.

To obtain a DHCPv4 Public ip dhcp option IPv6-only-preferred 1549 address, the IPv6-only-preferred command must be disabled, and the DHCPv4 client must be restarted. This command is disabled by default. NOTE Ensure the server supports the IPv6-only-preferred mode option, as the command will not work unless supported by the server. This reset eliminates the hop limit from the RAs that originate on the interface, so the host determines the hop limit. hop-limit <HOPLIMIT> Specifies the hop limit. Range: 0-255. Default: 64. Configures the MTU size to be sent in the RA messages. The no form of this command sets hop limit to the default value. Specifies the MTU size. Range: 1280-65535 bytes. Default: 1 500 bytes. <MTU-VALUE> Configures the ND time in milliseconds between DAD neighbor solicitations sent for an unresolved destination. Increase the ns-interval time if the network is slow or if there are persistent retry failures. Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command do?

The ip irdp holdtime <TIME> command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. The no form of this command removes the specified maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives and update it to the default value. Specifies the lifetime of router advertisements sent from this in terface. Range: 4 to 9000 seconds. Syntax: no ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

The no form of this command removes the specified minimum amount of time the switch waits between sending router advertisements and reverts to the default value. messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command do?

The aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators command enables/disables privilege elevation for the users in an administrators group. When enabled, an administrative user is provided lower privilege access upon successful switch login. The user must execute an enable command and re-authenticate to elevate their privilege to administrator (the administrator level access with ‘#’ prompt). Executing the disable command revokes an administrator privilege and returns the user to the lower privilege access level (the operator level access ‘>’ prompt). The no form of this command disables privilege elevation for the administrator user group. Syntax: no aaa authentication login privilege-elevation group administrators.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear mdns-sd statistics command do?

The clear mdns-sd statistics command clears all mDNS gateway statistics. Adds a filter rule to the service profile. The sequence number configured determines the priority with which the rule is matched. Lower the sequence number, higher is the priority. Following are the filter match parameters: • Service-name: mDNS packets are matched against the service IDs configured under the service name. • Service-instance-name: mDNS packets are matched against the service instance name present in the mDNS packets. When no match criteria is specified in the rule, then the rule can be matched against any mDNS packet. Once the match is found then either the packet can be permitted or denied based on the action specified in the rule. The no form of this command deletes the filter configured in the service profile. Syntax: clear mdns-sd statistics.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping command this command configures the drop unknown mode. While MLD snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that initiate an MLD request for multicast. Drop unknown mode can be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (exclusive-vlan). The default configuration is vlan-shared. The no form of this command configures the drop unknown mode on the switch to the default vlan-shared. Required: Enable shared VLAN filter on the switch. vlan-exclusive Required: Enable exclusive drop unknown filter per VLAN. This command delays the refresh for some IGMP or MLD protocol-related values. When this command is enabled, IGMP/MLD last_reporter value, source, group, or querier uptime, and the create time and expiry time values will be updated based on the configured timer values. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.

Public IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list 1909 The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Specifies the name of the ACL. <ACL-NAME> NOTE If the access list is configured for both L2 VLA N and L3 VLAN, the L3 VLAN configuration will be applied. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command enable MLD snooping on all VLANs. This configuration setting enables MLD snooping on all existing VLANs, as well as on any VLANs that are created after the IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans command is issued. When MLD snooping is enabled globally, MLD snooping can not be configured on individual VLANs within the VLAN context of the command-line interface. The no form of this command disables global MLD snooping, allows MLD snooping to remain enabled at the VLAN level, and allows the configuration of VLAN-level MLD snooping settings. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping all-vlans.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all command this command enables AOS-CX leaf devices (access-layer switches that connect directly to servers, storage, and other endpoints) to unconditionally forward multicast traffic to all VTEPs that do not support the exchange of MLD reports over the fabric. By default, when MLD snooping is enabled in AOS-CX, multicast traffic is selectively forwarded only to VTEPs connected to have interested hosts. This is achieved by exhanging MLD reports over VXLAN tunnels where each VTEP processes the remote reports and uses the membership information to forward multicast traffic efficiently, conserving fabric bandwidth. Syntax: no IPv6 mld snooping vxlan replicate-all.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip pim-sparse datapath-auto-include command do?

The ip pim-sparse datapath-auto-include command enables the router to forward multicast data received on the VXLAN L3VNI fabric to this interface, regardless of whether a multicast join was received on this interface or not. This allows the interface to be in the same multicast data path state on both the VSX peers. This command must be enabled on the VSX VXLAN routers to support multicast receivers over ROP or P2P SVI extensions. This command can be enabled on a transit L3 peering between VSX peers. This command is optional when the uplink is an MCLAGsVI. The no form of the command disables forwarding of multicast data on the interface. NOTE • An IP address must be configured on the interface and pim-sparse must be enabled. • This command must be enabled only on one interface per VRF. Syntax: no ip pim-sparse datapath-auto-include.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show IPv6 mroute command do?

Displays the information about PIM interfaces currently configured in the router for the given VRF. If VRF is not given, it displays for default VRF. messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ip ospf lsdb command do?

The show ip ospf lsdb command shows the OSPF link state database summary for different OSPF LSAs (Link State Advertisement). Use the parameters to get information for a particular LSA. Enter an OSPF process ID to display general OSPF information for a particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> adv-router {<ROUTER-ID>| self} Select to display link states for a particular advertising router. S pecify either a Router ID of the advertising router or specify sel f to show self-originated link states. area <AREA-ID> Select to display information filtered for the specified area in on e of the following formats. • OSPF area identifier in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a d ecimal number from 0 to 255. • OSPF area identifier in decimal format. Value: 0 to 4294967 295. Syntax: show ip ospf [<process-id>] lsdb.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 pim6-dense propagation-delay command do?

The IPv6 pim6-dense propagation-delay command configures the propagation delay that gets inserted into the LAN prune delay field of a LAN Prune Delay option. The no form of this command removes currently configured value and sets to the default of 500 ms. Specifies the propagation delay value in ms. Default: 500 ms. R ange: 250-2000. <DELAY-VALUE> Configures the multicast datagram time-to-live (router hop-count) threshold for the interface. Any IP multicast datagrams or state-refresh packets with a TTL less than this threshold will not be forwarded out the interface. The no form of this command removes the currently configured value and sets to the default of 3 attempts. Public IPv6 pim6-dense ttl-threshold 2751 Specifies the time-to-live threshold. Default: 3 attempts. Ran ge: 0-255. Syntax: IPv6 pim6-dense propagation-delay <DELAY-VALUE>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show IPv6 pim6 rpf (PIM-DM) command do?

If no options are specified, it shows information for the default VRF. <IP-ADDRESS> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> vsx-peer Optional. Specifies an IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxx x: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number f rom 0 to F. Shows PIM RPF details for the given IP address. Optional. Shows PIM interface information for all VRFs. Optional. Shows PIM interface information for a particular VRF. I f the <VRF-NAME> is not specified, it shows the default VRF i nformation. messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source command do?

The ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source command enables the router to accept multicast traffic from remote sources. When enabled, the router accepts multicast traffic from sources that are not directly connected and acts as the First Hop Router (FHR), performing registration to the Rendezvous Point (RP). In scenarios where a Gateway BD is configured in EVPN OISM networks, this should be applied to the external router interface that connects to the Gateway BD. The no form of this command removes the configuration on the interface and sets it to the default configuration. Syntax:
- no ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source
- ip pim-sparse accept-remote-source (IPv4) 2807.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip pim-sparse bfd command do?

The ip pim-sparse bfd command configures BFD on a per-interface basis for one interface associated with the PIM process. The no form of this command removes the BFD configuration on the interface and sets it to the default configuration. NOTE If BFD is enabled globally, it will be enabled by default on all interfaces. The only exception is when it is disabled specifically on an interface using the ip pim-sparse bfd disable command. If BFD is disabled globally, it will be disabled by default on all interfaces. The only exception is when it is enabled specifically on an interface using the ip pim-sparse bfd command. Disables the BFD configuration on the interface. Syntax: ip pim-sparse bfd [disable].

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME> command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: no crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. Public crypto pki certificate 3056 The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Specifies the name of a leaf certificate. Range: 1 to 32 alphanu meric characters (excluding "). <CERT-NAME>. Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows 802.1X reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users already authenticated retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables the cached reauthentication on a port. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth.

The no form of the command resets the discovery period to the default, 30 seconds. Specifies the discovery period (in seconds). Default: 30. Range: 1 to 65535. <PERIOD> Public aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator discovery-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Enables and configures the global password that the switch must use for MAC authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME> command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for MAC authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: no aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth 3191 The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD> Clears the MAC authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator state machines associated to this port. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has one to eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. <NAME> Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). Public qos queue-profile 3305. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command do?

The radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command specifies the number of seconds to wait for a response from the RADIUS server before trying the next RADIUS server. If a value is not specified, a default value of 5 seconds is used. You can override this value with a fine-grained per server timeout configured for individual servers. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS global authentication timeout to the default of 5 seconds. Specifies the timeout interval of 1 to 60 seconds. Default: 5 sec onds. Syntax: no radius-server timeout [<1-60>].

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIP process if not already created and enters the router rip <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.

NOTE • 8325, 8325H, 8325P, and 10000 Switch series require an internal VLAN for every subinterface, in addition to the internal VLANs used for other purposes. messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command do?

The ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command enables SSH enforcement that the username must be present in the certificate that is being used for authorization. This configuration alters how certificate-based authentication maps to a user account. When this is enabled, SSH will not require local user association with an authorized-key and instead enforces that the username used to log in is present within the certificate. The SSH server will check for the username in certificate fields Common Name or User Principle Name for a match. If a certificate is not used for authentication then this configuration has no effect on SSH authentication. The no form of this command disables the SSH enforcement of username in the certificate. Public ssh certificate-as-authorized-key 4088. Syntax: no ssh certificate-as-authorized-key.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. Public vlan (interface) 4175 If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4040. <VLAN-LIST> Creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx shutdown-on-split command do?

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync nd-snooping command do?

The vsx-sync nd-snooping command enables VSX synchronization of ND snooping configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize ND snooping configurations associated with a particular VLAN and interface, configure the same VLAN and interface on the peer device. When RA guard policy is enabled, this command also synchronizes RA guard policy related configurations. The no form of this command disables syncing ND snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync nd-snooping.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync neighbor command do?

The vsx-sync neighbor command enables VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. If any new modification or additional configuration is made on the primary node for IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration, they will be auto-synced. The no form of this command VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Public vsx-sync neighbor 4377. Syntax: no vsx-sync neighbor.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync ospf command do?

The vsx-sync ospf command enables syncing of OSPF (including OSPFv2 and OPSFv3), route map, and key chain configurations on the primary VSX switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize OSPF configurations at the port level context, configure the same port on the peer device. The no form of this command disables syncing of OSPF, route map, and key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE The OSPF router ID is not synchronized. This exclusion is needed because the router ID uniquely identifies the router. The two OSPF routers with the same router ID do not form an adjacency between them. Syntax: no vsx-sync ospf.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync qos-global command do?

The vsx-sync qos-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, such as CoS map, DSCP map, and trust policy, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync qos command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, but it does not remove the existing global QoS feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax:
- no vsx-sync qos-global
- vsx-sync qos-global 4381.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync route-map command do?

The vsx-sync route-map command enables syncing of all As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from the secondary to primary peer. The no form of this command disables syncing of As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE When vsx-sync for BGP or OSPF is configured, route map configurations are synchronized from primary VSX node to the secondary peer due to the dependency in configurations. Syntax: no vsx-sync route-map.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync vrrp command do?

The vsx-sync vrrp command enables VSX synchronization of all VRRP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize VRRP configurations at the port level context, the same port must be configured on the peer device with IP address. The no form of this command disables syncing VRRP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. NOTE BFD IP is the IP address of VRRP peer device. Hence it cannot be synced. In the owner scenario, in case the priority is synced, both VSX primary and secondary devices will have 255 as their priority. Syntax: no vsx-sync vrrp.

The erase all zeroize command restores the switch to its factory default configuration. You will be prompted before the procedure starts. Once complete, the switch will restart from the primary image with factory default settings. Syntax: erase [all] zeroize.
